label: Supabase Auth SSR
summary: Use when the site EXPLICITLY needs Supabase Auth in a Next.js App Router app: cookie-based SSR sessions, middleware session refresh, a server client, a browser client, and an OAuth code-exchange callback route. Do NOT use as a generic auth default when a non-Supabase provider is intended — plain login/auth intent routes to the 'auth' capability (clerk-auth) instead.

envVars.purpose: Supabase project URL used by the browser, server, middleware and callback clients (Supabase project → API settings). Tagged 'feature-runtime': auth cannot authenticate anyone without a real value, but the dossier degrades gracefully at runtime (middleware passes through, UI shows an 'Auth ej konfigurerat' notice via isSupabaseAuthConfigured) instead of crashing — so F3 reports a missing value as a warning, not a build blocker.

Supabase anon public API key used by the browser, server, middleware and callback clients (Supabase project → API settings). Tagged 'feature-runtime' for the same reason as NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: the code degrades to an unconfigured-auth notice at runtime instead of throwing, so it is a runtime warning rather than a hard build blocker.
